name: Smoking Salmon and Trout Part IX - Indian or Hard Smoked
recipeIngredient: Low-fat fish (makes the longest keeping hard smoked fish; fat causes rancidity)
Medium-fat hard smoked fish should be frozen or salted until just before eating
Lean fish will keep indefinitely unrefrigerated
Hard smoked fish can be made from fresh, frozen, or hard salted fish
recipeInstructions:
1. For hard salted fish: Freshen the fish before smoking (24 to 48 hours with water changes every 3 to 6 hours)
- Ensure no salty taste remains after drying concentrates any remaining saltiness
2. Brine preparation for fresh fish and thawed frozen fish (if needed):
- Make a 90°F brine solution
- Soak times based on thickness:
- 1/4" thickness: 2 minutes
- 1/2" thickness: 4 minutes
- 3/4" thickness: 7 minutes
- 1 inch: 10 minutes
- 1 1/2 inches: 15 minutes
- 2 inches: 20 minutes
3. Smoking:
- Smoke for only a portion of the total drying period according to taste preference
- Dry at 85°F for 30 hours using a forced draft smoker, up to 3 weeks with natural draft conditions depending on weather until completely dry and hard
(Note: Extracted from "Smoking Salmon & Trout" by Jack Whelan, published by Airie Publishing. ISBN: 0-919807-00-3)
